cards.hook: 3,200 gaming jobs gone — zero studios closed
cards.caption: 3,200 jobs cut, 4 studios under new management, zero closed on paper. The layoff math the industry doesn't spell out. The signal my feed keeps before the timeline spins it.
cards.stance: "No studios closed" is a PR line when 3,200 people just lost their jobs.

cards.hook: Pokemon just got its own official soccer team
cards.caption: Pikachu now reps Japan's J.League — all 60 clubs. The crossover you didn't see coming and won't see on your timeline for days. The kind of drop my feed surfaces first.

cards.hook: Xbox gutted the Elder Scrolls Online team
cards.caption: ESO's team seemingly gutted in the layoffs — but the roadmap 'will be shifting,' not dying. What that actually means for the MMO you play. Found before the timeline caught up.
cards.stance: 'Roadmaps will be shifting' is how you say a live game is on life support without saying it.

cards.hook: Gaming industry isn't taking a break from bad news
Microsoft still thinks AAA gaming is a growth industry
cards.caption: MS is betting AAA is still growth, not just aging millennial fans. The bet driving every Xbox layoff and reshuffle you're watching. The take my feed keeps before it's obvious.
cards.stance: Treating AAA as a growth market in 2026 is how you justify the cuts that kill it.

cards.hook: Xbox CEO wants games for 'a billion people a day'
cards.caption: Asha Sharma's target: 24x Steam's peak population — daily. Ambition or delusion? The quote fueling the whole Xbox strategy debate. Surfaced before the dunks pile up.
cards.stance: A billion players a day is 24x Steam's peak — that's not a strategy, it's a fantasy.

cards.hook: As the gaming industry gets worse
Hearing about layoffs at this scale in sales and gaming
Layoffs aren't about DEI — stop pretending
Sony is gutting gaming while you watch Microsoft
cards.caption: Sony is gutting gaming while everyone watches Microsoft. The timing isn't a coincidence — it's cover. Industry moves like this surface in my feed before the timeline catches on.
cards.stance: The Microsoft news is a smokescreen — Sony picked this exact week on purpose.

cards.hook: GDC is fading. Gamescom is eating its lunch.
cards.caption: GDC is fading while Gamescom goes from strength to strength. The center of gravity in gaming events is shifting — this is the kind of signal my feed keeps and the timeline misses.

cards.hook: Warframe's studio is building a dark-fantasy world next
cards.caption: Soulframe just dropped its TennoLive 2026 teaser — the Warframe studio's dark-fantasy swing. If you love Digital Extremes, this is your next obsession. Found before the timeline.

cards.hook: Xbox laid off staff — then said it wants to 'entertain a billion daily'
cards.caption: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma wants a billion people entertained daily — in the same memo about layoffs. The disconnect is the story. The stuff my feed catches, timelines gloss over.
cards.stance: 'Entertain a billion daily' in a layoff memo isn't vision — it's tone-deaf.

cards.hook: Assassin's Creed Black Flag is getting resynced
cards.caption: AC Black Flag Resynced lands July 9 on PS5 and Xbox — the pirate era, back. If Black Flag was your favorite Assassin's Creed, mark the date. Caught before the timeline.

cards.hook: The Shadow of the Colossus creator's new game is Xbox-first
cards.caption: Fumito Ueda — ICO, Shadow of the Colossus, The Last Guardian — just revealed gen ATLAS, an open-world action-adventure coming first to Xbox. This is the reveal my feed kept.

cards.hook: Nintendo isn't the good guy fans think it is
A trillion-dollar company just gutted its gamers
cards.caption: A trillion-dollar company btw. The people making gaming decisions aren't even gamers anymore, and the industry's feeling it. Raw take my feed pulled before the timeline buried it.
cards.stance: The biggest threat to games isn't AI — it's non-gamers running gaming companies.

cards.hook: Gaming news has sucked for a week straight
PlayStation kills game discs in January 2028
cards.caption: PlayStation stops making physical discs in Jan 2028 — and the industry is not taking it well. If you still own your games, this one's for you. Caught it before the timeline did.
cards.stance: Digital-only means you never actually own the games you paid for.

cards.hook: Xbox hit with major layoffs and studio spin-offs
Xbox just cut 3,200 people — its biggest ever
cards.caption: 3,200 gone across FY27, 1,600 today — the biggest restructure in Xbox history. Compulsion and Double Fine spun off, Ninja Theory sold. The numbers my feed surfaced before the headlines.
cards.stance: Record profits and record layoffs in the same quarter tells you who games are really for.

cards.hook: Suits call it 'a transformation.' Devs call it a fire.
cards.caption: Content creators: 'no worries, it's just a transformation.' Actual devs: 'the industry is ON FIRE, who makes the games now?' The gap between spin and reality my feed keeps.
cards.stance: 'Transformation' is corporate for 'we fired the people who make the thing.'

cards.hook: An edit that nails how absurd gaming got
New Metroid leaked from a Brazilian ratings board
cards.caption: New Metroid leaked from a Brazilian ratings board. "Ravenous" surfaced before any official reveal. Rating filings always leak first — the stuff my feed catches before the trailer drops.
cards.stance: Ratings boards leak more games than Nintendo ever announces on purpose.

cards.hook: Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 is officially real
cards.caption: Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 is officially real. Cold Iron and Daybreak revealed it as IGN's cover story. If you loved the co-op original, this one's for you — found before the timeline.

cards.hook: Sony's PS disc factory is already being repurposed
cards.caption: Sony's PS disc factory is already being repurposed, and Microsoft's lining up buyers for Xbox studios. Physical media and the console era are quietly ending — the signal my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The disc era is over and nobody's holding a funeral.

cards.hook: "Indie gaming will save the industry" is a myth
cards.caption: "Indie gaming will save the industry" is a myth, and one dev just said it out loud. The comforting takes rarely survive scrutiny. The contrarian threads my feed keeps before they blow up.
cards.stance: Indies are a genre, not a rescue plan for a $200B industry.

cards.hook: 15 biggest game reveals from July 2026, in one place
cards.caption: 15 biggest game reveals from July 2026, in one place. Every major announcement and gameplay drop from the past few weeks. Skip the doomscroll — the roundup my feed built for you.
